channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: New York Times reports sexual abuse allegations against Cesar Chavez
item.description: A New York Times investigation has revealed allegations that the late renowned labor leader Cesar Chavez abused girls and raped Dolores Huerta, his longtime organizing partner.
item.content:encoded: A New York Times investigation has revealed allegations that the late renowned labor leader Cesar Chavez abused girls and raped Dolores Huerta, his longtime organizing partner.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Florido

item.title: How chess became king in South Texas
item.description: South Texas is the chess capital of the state, due in large part to the University of Texas Rio Grande Valley. Its chess team is preparing to defend its national title in competitive collegiate chess.
item.content:encoded: South Texas is the chess capital of the state, due in large part to the University of Texas Rio Grande Valley. Its chess team is preparing to defend its national title in competitive collegiate chess.
item.dc:creator: Ana Campbell

item.title: Cesar Chavez abused and raped women and girls, NYT investigation says
item.description: A New York Times investigation has revealed allegations that the late renowned labor leader abused girls and raped Dolores Huerta, his longtime organizing partner.
item.content:encoded:
A New York Times investigation has revealed allegations that the late renowned labor leader abused girls and raped Dolores Huerta, his longtime organizing partner.

(Image credit: George Brich)
item.dc:creator: Adrian Florido

item.title: A mom wrote a kids' book on grief. She was just convicted of her husband's murder
item.description: A Utah jury convicted Kouri Richins of fatally spiking her husband's drink with fentanyl in 2022. Prosecutors said she was hoping to collect millions of dollars from multiple life insurance policies.
item.content:encoded:
A Utah jury convicted Kouri Richins of fatally spiking her husband's drink with fentanyl in 2022. Prosecutors said she was hoping to collect millions of dollars from multiple life insurance policies.

(Image credit: Rick Bowmer)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Mamdani put Ramadan at the center of NYC's cultural life, bringing joy -- and a backlash
item.description: NYC Mayor Mamdani observed Ramadan publicly at a time when many politicians and activists on the right are voicing hostility and in some cases open bigotry toward American Muslims.
item.content:encoded:
NYC Mayor Mamdani observed Ramadan publicly at a time when many politicians and activists on the right are voicing hostility and in some cases open bigotry toward American Muslims.

(Image credit: Brian Mann)
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: FAA tightens safety rules for helicopters and planes around major airports
item.description: Regulators at the Federal Aviation Administration are tightening safety rules in congested airspace around major airports, suspending the use of visual separation between planes and helicopters.
item.content:encoded:
Regulators at the Federal Aviation Administration are tightening safety rules in congested airspace around major airports, suspending the use of visual separation between planes and helicopters.

(Image credit: Tom Brenner)
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: Trump temporarily waives the Jones Act to try to lower gasoline prices. Will it work?
item.description: The Jones Act restricts which ships can carry goods between U.S. ports. Experts say temporarily lifting the act will do little to affect gas prices.
item.content:encoded:
The Jones Act restricts which ships can carry goods between U.S. ports. Experts say temporarily lifting the act will do little to affect gas prices.

(Image credit: Damian Dovarganes)
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise

item.title: A mysterious floral artist has taken over the New York Botanical Garden
item.description: Mr. Flower Fantastic is a graffiti artist turned floral designer who keeps his identity a secret. His new show is an ode to NYC in orchids. Oh, and did we mention he's allergic to flowers?
item.content:encoded:
Mr. Flower Fantastic is a graffiti artist turned floral designer who keeps his identity a secret. His new show is an ode to NYC in orchids. Oh, and did we mention he's allergic to flowers?

(Image credit: New York Botanical Garden)
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Vanasco

item.title: The threats to Minnesota's Medicaid funds are unprecedented. Other states could be next
item.description: Hundreds of millions of dollars — and possibly billions — for the state's Medicaid program are in limbo as part of the Trump administration's crackdown on fraud.
item.content:encoded:
Hundreds of millions of dollars — and possibly billions — for the state's Medicaid program are in limbo as part of the Trump administration's crackdown on fraud.

(Image credit: Anna Moneymaker)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: ICE's detention expansion meets resistance in communities across the political spectrum
item.description: Last year ICE got $45 billion from Congress over four years to expand its detention footprint. Many towns and cities are pushing back.
item.content:encoded:
Last year ICE got $45 billion from Congress over four years to expand its detention footprint. Many towns and cities are pushing back. 

(Image credit: Wesley Lapointe)
item.dc:creator: Jasmine Garsd
